title: Tesla Plans to Double Austin Robotaxi Fleet to ~60 by December
summary: Elon Musk announced plans to double the Austin Robotaxi fleet from 29-30 current vehicles to about 60 next month, far short of the prior 500-vehicle end-2025 target. All current Austin rides remain supervised with safety drivers.
impact_reason: Promises fleet growth but remains supervised and well below targets, resulting in neutral net effect on unsupervised scaling velocity in Austin.

title: Austin Unsupervised Robotaxi Fleet at 13 Active Vehicles
summary: Robotaxi Tracker reports 13 unsupervised vehicles active in Austin's geofence over the last 30 days as of April 20.
impact_reason: Indicates steady low-scale unsupervised operations in Austin, maintaining baseline fleet presence without rapid growth.

title: Tesla Expands Unsupervised Robotaxi to Dallas and Houston
summary: Tesla launched unsupervised Robotaxi service in Dallas and Houston on April 18, with initial geofence areas in suburban zones of each city and video evidence showing no safety driver. This triples Texas operations alongside ongoing Austin scaling.
impact_reason: Establishes new unsupervised hubs in Texas, directly accelerating overall state-wide fleet scaling and data collection momentum despite small initial deployments.

title: Tesla Deploys Minimal Fleet in Dallas and Houston Launches
summary: Crowdsourced tracking data shows Tesla deployed only one unsupervised vehicle in each of Dallas and Houston at launch, expanding to two vehicles per city by Tuesday morning, compared to 13 active unsupervised vehicles in Austin. The Bay Area fleet remains significantly larger at over 500 vehicles.
impact_reason: While geographically significant, the extremely limited vehicle deployment (2 per city) reveals slow absolute scaling velocity and suggests Austin remains the primary unsupervised testing ground, making this a neutral operational reality check rather than a meaningful acceleration catalyst.

title: TechCrunch reports Tesla Robotaxi launch in Dallas and Houston
summary: Tesla's Robotaxi service launched in Dallas and Houston with video evidence of unsupervised driving, now operating in three Texas cities after Austin. Crowdsourced data logs one vehicle each in the new cities versus 46 in Austin.
impact_reason: Provides independent verification of unsupervised service expansion in Texas, building on Austin's operations and signaling potential for fleet growth in the region.

title: Tesla Austin Unsupervised Fleet Reaches 10 Model Ys
summary: Tesla's Austin robotaxi fleet achieved 10 unsupervised Model Y vehicles, marking the highest confirmed count of driverless vehicles in the service.
impact_reason: Mildly positive incremental progress in Austin unsupervised fleet growth, but the modest absolute number (10 vehicles) remains far below stated targets and demonstrates slow scaling velocity for the flagship market.
